Professor View 01 / 27
Travel English -- 48 Aulas

Andrea Aggio

Conversação para Feiras Internacionais de Turismo & Hotelaria

B2 (Intermediário-Avançado) São Paulo, SP Diretora Comercial — Escape Turismo 90 min · Online
Progresso Geral0%
Velocidade:
Nome

Andrea Aggio

Idade

56 anos

Cidade

São Paulo, SP

Profissão

Diretora Comercial — Escape Turismo (agência de viagens)

Nível

B2 (Intermediário-Avançado) — base sólida adormecida, reativação de fluência

Foco

Conversação: Travel & Hospitality — feiras internacionais, networking com hoteleiros, fluência e espontaneidade

Frequência

Semanal (quintas, 18h) — Online (Zoom)

Total de Aulas

15 aulas (90 min)

Personagem-Jornada

De: Sabe que tem inglês dentro de si — anos de estudo, CCAA, dois meses em San Diego — mas trava na hora de falar, especialmente nas feiras internacionais, quando mais precisa. O afastamento prolongado criou um bloqueio de fluência que a incomoda profissionalmente diante de hoteleiros e fornecedores estrangeiros.

Para: Consegue conversar com fluência e naturalidade com fornecedores e hoteleiros internacionais, sem depender de script — "jogar conversa fora" em inglês com qualquer pessoa do setor, em qualquer feira, em qualquer país. Em 15 quintas-feiras.

Promessa Transformadora

De travar nas feiras internacionais a "jogar conversa fora" em inglês com qualquer hoteleiro do mundo. O desafio da Andrea não é começar do zero — a base existe. É reativar a fluência e a espontâneidade sob pressão real: responder sem hesitar, puxar conversa, acessar o vocabulário na hora certa. Cada aula usa o mundo dela — feiras, hotelaria, viagens, atualidades — para que o inglês volte a sair sozinho.

Forças
  • Base linguística sólida e adormecida (colégio, CCAA, imersão em San Diego)
  • Contexto profissional riquíssimo: turismo, feiras, hotelaria internacional
  • Autoconhecimento pedagógico: sabe que funciona com professor fixo e conversação
  • Alta motivação — a dor é concreta e recorrente (feiras 2-3x por ano)
  • Tolerância ao erro alta: trata o afastamento com pragmatismo, sem culpa
Pontos de Melhoria
  • Fluência oral e espontaneidade — o principal bloqueio declarado
  • Acesso rápido ao vocabulário sob pressão (feiras, networking)
  • Precisão nos tempos verbais ao falar da carreira (present perfect vs past)
  • Consistência de frequência — rotina carregada e viagens frequentes
  • Listening para sotaques não nativos de parceiros internacionais

Currículo Completo — 15 Aulas

#TemaFoco LinguísticoAtividade PrincipalStatus
1Reactivation Diagnostic — Who Are You in English?Present perfect vs present simple vs past simpleAuto-apresentação e conversa na feiraAtual
2Back in the Game — Reactivating Your English IdentityPresent perfect for experience + for/sinceContar a trajetória em turismoPróxima
3The Art of Small Talk at International FairsQuestion forms + conversation openersPuxar e sustentar conversa na feiraPróxima
4Talking About Your World — Trips & HighlightsPast simple vs present perfect (narrar)Descrever viagens e experiênciasPróxima
5What's Next — Plans, Goals & CommitmentsFuture forms (will / going to / present continuous)Falar de planos e metas profissionaisPróxima
6Making It Stick — Networking & Follow-UpsPolite requests + follow-up languageConstruir relacionamentos e dar sequênciaPróxima
7Listening and Responding to Native SpeakersListening strategies + clarifying questionsEntender e responder em tempo realPróxima
8Midpoint Review — How Far Has Andrea Come?Consolidação do Bloco 1Assessment real e simulação de feiraPróxima
9Talking Prices, Packages & DealsNumbers, comparatives & negotiation languageNegociar pacotes e condiçõesPróxima
10Being Yourself in English — Opinion & PersonalityOpinion language + linkersDar opinião em conversa semi-formalPróxima
11Holding the Floor at International Trade ShowsTurn-taking + fillers & discourse markersManter a conversa fluindoPróxima
12Describing Trips & Destinations — StorytellingDescriptive language + narrative tensesContar histórias como ferramenta comercialPróxima
13Handling Difficult Moments & RecoveryDiplomatic language + clarifying & apologizingContornar mal-entendidos e reclamaçõesPróxima
14Small Talk Mastery — Genuine ConnectionRegister, empathy & active listeningDe cortesia a conexão verdadeiraPróxima
15Finding Your Voice — Fluency & ConfidenceIntegração de todas as estruturasConversa livre e re-gravação do baselinePróxima

Metodologia

1. Reativação, não Ensino do Zero

A base existe. Cada aula acorda o inglês adormecido da Andrea com input rico e muita produção oral, transformando conhecimento passivo em fala ativa e espontânea.

2. Contexto Real de Turismo & Feiras

Todo conteúdo usa o mundo dela: feiras internacionais, hoteleiros, fornecedores, roteiros, negociações. Ela pratica exatamente as conversas que vai ter de verdade.

3. Fluência Sob Pressão

Role-plays graduados (guided → semi-free → free) recriam a pressão das feiras, com imprevistos, para que a espontaneidade resista mesmo no calor da conversa.

4. Ritmo Dinâmico (50+)

Mudança de atividade a cada 15-20 minutos, sem silêncios longos ou exercícios mecânicos. A experiência de vida da Andrea é recurso pedagógico central.

5. Correção Adiada e Diagnóstica

Na conversa, fluência primeiro; correção depois. O professor anota padrões recorrentes e devolve feedback focado, sem interromper a produção.

Mapa de Personalidade

IntrovertidaExtrovertida
Aprendiz leitura/escritaAprendiz auditiva/oral
ImpulsivaReflexiva
Tolerância ao erro baixaTolerância ao erro alta
Energia caóticaEnergia calma e estruturada
Insegura em inglêsConfiante em inglês
Foco acadêmicoFoco profissional
Aula 01 -- Pre-class

Reactivation Diagnostic -- Who Are You in English?

Reactivating your English at an international tourism fair. Key words: hospitality, trade fair, hotelier, supplier, itinerary, booking, venue, destination, to network, lead, to reach out, to follow up. Structures: present perfect (experience & duration with for/since) vs present simple (current role) vs past simple (finished time).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Hospitality -- the business of hotels and welcoming guests (hotelaria, hospitalidade)
"I have worked in hospitality for over twenty years."
Trade fair -- a big event where companies show their services (feira do setor)
"Every year, I go to two international trade fairs."
Hotelier -- a person who owns or manages a hotel (hoteleiro)
"I met a hotelier from Munich at the fair."
Supplier -- a company that provides goods or services (fornecedor)
"We are looking for a reliable local supplier."
Itinerary -- a detailed plan of a trip (roteiro de viagem)
"These hotels would be perfect for our itineraries."
Booking -- a reservation for a room or a tour (reserva)
"We can talk about bookings for next season."
Venue -- a place where an event is held (local do evento)
"We have just opened two new venues on the coast."
Destination -- a place that people travel to (destino)
"Right now, we are expanding our European destinations."
To network -- to meet people and build relationships (fazer contatos, networking)
"At the fair, I network with hoteliers worldwide."
Lead -- a possible new client or opportunity (contato, oportunidade de negócio)
"I came back from Berlin with ten strong leads."
To reach out -- to contact someone to start a conversation (entrar em contato)
"Please reach out whenever you have a moment."
To follow up -- to contact someone again after a meeting (dar sequência, retomar contato)
"I will follow up next week to talk about the details."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Hospitality
Trade fair
Hotelier
Supplier
Itinerary
Booking
Venue
Destination
To network
Lead
To reach out
To follow up

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Andrea is the Commercial Director at Escape Turismo, a travel agency in Brazil. She runs the commercial team and works with hoteliers and suppliers around the world. "I have worked in hospitality for over twenty years," she says. When she was young, she lived in San Diego for two months, but then she stopped using English for a long time. Now she is back. Every year, she goes to two international trade fairs to network and find new leads. Last week, at the fair in Berlin, she met a hotelier from Munich. Right now, her team is expanding their European destinations, and they have just signed two new venues on the coast. Next week, Andrea will reach out to follow up and talk about bookings.

1. Por que dizemos "I have worked in hospitality for over twenty years"?
A Porque a experiência começou no passado e continua até agora (present perfect + for).
B Porque a ação terminou e não tem mais ligação com o presente.
C Porque é uma rotina que ela faz toda semana.
2. "When she was young, she lived in San Diego" usa o past simple porque:
A A experiência continua até hoje.
B É uma ação terminada, num tempo terminado do passado.
C É algo que está acontecendo agora.
3. Which sentence about her current role is correct?
A "She has run the commercial team since twenty years."
B "She runs the commercial team at Escape Turismo."
C "She is running the commercial team every day."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Perfect vs Present Simple vs Past Simple

GRAMMAR

Como falar da sua carreira com o tempo verbal certo (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
Present Perfect
have / has + past participle		Experiência e duração até agora. Experience and duration up to now (for / since).I have worked here for 20 years.
just + present perfectNotícia muito recente. Very recent news.We have just opened two venues.
Present Simple
I / you / we + verb (he/she + s)		Papel atual e rotina. Current role and routine.I run the commercial team.
Past Simple
verb + -ed		Ação terminada, tempo terminado. Finished action, finished time (in 2005).I lived in San Diego in my twenties.
for vs sincefor + período (for 20 years) · since + ponto de partida (since 2004)

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"I in tourism for over twenty years."
"Every year, I to two international fairs."
"Last year, I five new hotels in Portugal."
"We a new local supplier."
"I have worked in this company 2004."
"I have known this hotelier three years."

Stage 2: Put the Conversation in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de uma conversa na feira na ordem correta.

?Explain what you are looking for at the fair.
?Exchange cards and agree to follow up.
?Strike up a conversation and say "Nice to meet you."
?Suggest a meeting to talk about bookings.
?Introduce yourself: your name, role, and company.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

Hi, I am Andrea, Commercial Director at Escape Turismo.
Oi, sou a Andrea, Diretora Comercial na Escape Turismo.
I have worked in hospitality for over twenty years.
Trabalho com hotelaria há mais de vinte anos.
I am looking for new hotel partners for our itineraries.
Estou procurando novos parceiros hoteleiros para os nossos roteiros.
Right now, we are expanding our European destinations.
Agora, estamos expandindo os nossos destinos europeus.
I will reach out next week to follow up about bookings.
Vou entrar em contato na semana que vem para dar sequência sobre as reservas.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real na feira.

A hotelier asks: "So, what do you do?" You answer:
A "I do travel things for a company."
B "I am the Commercial Director at Escape Turismo. I run our commercial team."
C "I am working in tourism since twenty years."
He asks: "How long have you worked in tourism?" The best answer is:
A "I work in tourism for over twenty years."
B "I have worked in tourism for over twenty years."
C "I am working in tourism for over twenty years."
You want to mention a finished moment in your career. Which is correct?
A "I lived in San Diego when I was twenty."
B "I have lived in San Diego when I was twenty."
C "I am living in San Diego when I was twenty."
You want to end the conversation and keep the contact. You say:
A "It was great to meet you. I will reach out next week to follow up."
B "Okay, bye, we finish now."
C "I am reach out to you since next week."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine you are at an international tourism fair. A hotelier walks up and asks: "So, what do you do? Tell me about your company." Introduce yourself: your role (present simple), your experience (present perfect, for/since), and what you are looking for right now. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 1

1Hi, I am Andrea, Commercial Director at Escape Turismo.Oi, sou a Andrea, Diretora Comercial na Escape Turismo.
2I have worked in tourism for over twenty years.Trabalho com turismo há mais de vinte anos.
3I am looking for new hotel partners for our itineraries.Estou procurando novos parceiros hoteleiros para os nossos roteiros.
4It was great to meet you. Let me get your card.Foi ótimo te conhecer. Me dá o seu cartão.
5I will reach out next week to follow up.Vou entrar em contato na semana que vem para dar sequência.
Aula 02 -- Pre-class

Back in the Game -- Your Professional Identity

Describing your role and your current projects at the travel agency. Key words: manage, oversee, coordinate, handle, liaise with, be responsible for, travel agency, hospitality industry, hotel portfolio, incoming tourism, outbound travel, destination management. Structures: present simple (role & routine) vs present continuous (projects happening right now).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Manage -- to be in charge of a team or an area (gerenciar, chefiar)
"I manage a team of eight people."
Oversee -- to watch over work and make sure it is done well (supervisionar)
"I oversee every deal the agency signs."
Coordinate -- to organize people and tasks so they work together (coordenar)
"I coordinate the calendar for all our trips."
Handle -- to deal with a task, a client, or a problem (lidar com, cuidar de)
"I handle the biggest clients myself."
Liaise with -- to stay in contact and work closely with someone (articular com)
"I liaise with hoteliers across Europe."
Be responsible for -- to have the duty to take care of something (ser responsável por)
"I am responsible for the European itineraries."
Travel agency -- a company that plans and sells trips to clients (agência de viagens)
"I work for a travel agency in Brazil."
Hospitality industry -- the sector of hotels, restaurants and tourism (setor de hotelaria)
"The hospitality industry is growing fast."
Hotel portfolio -- the group of hotels a company works with (portfólio de hotéis)
"We are expanding our hotel portfolio."
Incoming tourism -- visitors who come into your country (turismo receptivo)
"Incoming tourism to Europe is booming."
Outbound travel -- travelers who go abroad from your country (turismo emissivo)
"We are launching a new outbound travel program."
Destination management -- organizing the services and experiences at a place (gestão de destinos)
"Destination management is a big part of our work."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Manage
Oversee
Coordinate
Handle
Liaise with
Be responsible for
Travel agency
Hospitality industry
Hotel portfolio
Incoming tourism
Outbound travel
Destination management

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Andrea is the Commercial Director at Escape Turismo, a travel agency in Brazil. Every week, she manages the commercial team, oversees the biggest deals, and liaises with hoteliers all over Europe. She is responsible for the European itineraries, so she coordinates the calendar and handles the key clients herself. This season, however, is different. Right now, the agency is going through fast growth. At the moment, her team is expanding the hotel portfolio in southern Europe and is launching a new outbound travel program for corporate clients. Andrea says the routine keeps the agency running, but the new projects keep it alive.

1. Why do we say "Every week, she manages the commercial team"?
A Because it is a routine and a permanent part of her role (present simple).
B Because it is happening only at this exact moment.
C Because it finished in the past and has no link to now.
2. "Right now, the agency is going through fast growth" uses the present continuous because:
A It is a permanent fact that is always true.
B It describes a temporary situation happening around now.
C It is a routine she repeats every week.
3. Which sentence about a current project is correct?
A At the moment, her team expands the hotel portfolio.
B At the moment, her team is expanding the hotel portfolio.
C At the moment, her team is expand the hotel portfolio.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Simple vs Present Continuous

GRAMMAR

Como falar do seu cargo (sempre) e dos seus projetos (agora) com o tempo verbal certo.

FormUse / UsoExample
Present Simple
verb (he/she + s)		Papel, rotina e fatos permanentes. Role, routine and permanent facts (usually, every year).I manage the commercial team.
Present Continuous
am / is / are + -ing		Projetos temporarios acontecendo agora. Temporary projects happening now (right now, at the moment).We are expanding our portfolio.
Signal wordsSimple: every day, usually, always · Continuous: right now, at the moment, currently, this season.
Stative verbsVerbos de estado (know, want, be) ficam no simple, nunca no continuous: I know, nao I am knowing.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"As Commercial Director, I a team of eight people."
"Right now, we our hotel portfolio."
"Every year, our agency with ten countries."
"At the moment, I on a new partnership."
"I many hoteliers in Europe."
"This season, our team a new program."

Stage 2: Put the Conversation in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de uma conversa profissional sobre o seu trabalho na ordem correta.

?Suggest a way your two companies could work together.
?Describe a project your team is working on right now.
?Ask the other person about their current work.
?Explain what you usually do in a typical week.
?Introduce yourself and your role at the agency.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

I am the Commercial Director, and I manage our commercial team.
Sou a Diretora Comercial e gerencio a nossa equipe comercial.
I am responsible for the European itineraries.
Sou responsável pelos roteiros europeus.
Right now, we are expanding our hotel portfolio.
Agora, estamos expandindo o nosso portfólio de hotéis.
At the moment, I am working on a new partnership in Italy.
No momento, estou trabalhando em uma nova parceria na Itália.
Part of my job is to liaise with hoteliers and suppliers.
Parte do meu trabalho é articular com hoteleiros e fornecedores.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

A partner asks: "What do you do at the agency?" The best answer is:
A I am managing the commercial team since ten years.
B I manage the commercial team and I am responsible for the European itineraries.
C I do commercial things for the agency.
A partner asks: "What is your team working on this season?" You answer:
A Right now, we are expanding our hotel portfolio in Europe.
B Right now, we expand our hotel portfolio in Europe.
C Right now, we are expand our hotel portfolio in Europe.
You want to describe a permanent fact about the agency. Which is correct?
A Our agency is working with ten countries every year.
B Our agency works with ten countries every year.
C Our agency work with ten countries every year.
You want to say a stative verb correctly. Choose the natural sentence:
A I am knowing many hoteliers in Europe.
B I know many hoteliers in Europe.
C I am know many hoteliers in Europe.

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine a new partner asks about your work. Describe your role using the present simple (what you manage, oversee, and are responsible for), and then describe two projects your team is working on right now using the present continuous. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 2

1I am the Commercial Director, and I manage our commercial team.Sou a Diretora Comercial e gerencio a nossa equipe comercial.
2I am responsible for our European itineraries.Sou responsável pelos nossos roteiros europeus.
3Right now, we are expanding our hotel portfolio in Europe.Agora, estamos expandindo o nosso portfólio de hotéis na Europa.
4At the moment, I am working on a new partnership.No momento, estou trabalhando em uma nova parceria.
5Part of my job is to liaise with hoteliers and suppliers.Parte do meu trabalho é articular com hoteleiros e fornecedores.
Aula 03 -- Pre-class

The Art of Small Talk -- Conversations at the Fair

Starting and sustaining conversations at international tourism fairs. Key words: small talk, icebreaker, conversation opener, common ground, follow-up question, to wrap up, booth, exhibitor, delegate, keynote speaker, badge, panel session. Structures: question formation (present simple, present continuous) and present perfect for experience (How long have you...?).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Small talk -- light, friendly conversation about everyday topics (bate-papo, conversa leve)
"A little small talk breaks the ice at any fair."
Icebreaker -- something you say to start a conversation comfortably (quebra-gelo)
"A good icebreaker is a simple question about the event."
Conversation opener -- a first line used to begin a chat (frase de abertura)
"My favorite conversation opener is: what brings you here?"
Common ground -- a shared interest or experience two people have (interesse em comum)
"We found common ground in our love of travel."
Follow-up question -- a question that keeps the conversation going (pergunta de continuidade)
"A follow-up question shows you are really listening."
To wrap up -- to bring a conversation to a friendly close (encerrar, finalizar)
"Let us wrap up and exchange cards."
Booth -- a small stand where a company shows its products at a fair (estande)
"Come by our booth this afternoon."
Exhibitor -- a company or person showing products at a fair (expositor)
"As an exhibitor, I meet dozens of delegates a day."
Delegate -- a person who attends a conference to represent a company (delegado, participante)
"Every delegate wears a badge at the fair."
Keynote speaker -- the main speaker at a conference (palestrante principal)
"The keynote speaker takes the stage at four."
Badge -- the name card you wear at an event (crachá)
"Please wear your badge at all times."
Panel session -- a talk where several experts discuss a topic (mesa-redonda, painel)
"The panel session on sustainable travel starts soon."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Small talk
Icebreaker
Conversation opener
Common ground
Follow-up question
To wrap up
Booth
Exhibitor
Delegate
Keynote speaker
Badge
Panel session

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

At the fair, a delegate named Daniel walks up to Andrea's booth. "Are you enjoying the fair so far?" he asks — a warm icebreaker. "I am, thanks," Andrea says. "It is my third time here. Is this your first ITB?" They quickly find common ground: both love hospitality. Andrea keeps the conversation going with a good follow-up question: "How long have you worked in hospitality?" Daniel is an exhibitor from Lisbon. "For about ten years," he says. "I have just launched a small hotel group. Have you seen the keynote speaker yet?" Before they wrap up, they exchange cards and agree to keep in touch.

1. Why does Daniel start with "Are you enjoying the fair?"
A Because it is a friendly icebreaker to start a conversation.
B Because he wants to end the conversation quickly.
C Because he is asking about a finished event in the past.
2. "How long have you worked in hospitality?" uses the present perfect because:
A It asks about something happening only right now.
B It asks about experience and duration up to now.
C It is a routine that repeats every week.
3. Which question is formed correctly?
A You are enjoying the fair?
B Are you enjoying the fair?
C Enjoying you the fair?

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Question Formation & Present Perfect

GRAMMAR

Como montar boas perguntas para abrir e sustentar uma conversa (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
Present Simple
Do / Does + subject + verb		Fatos e rotinas. Facts and routines.Do you come here often?
Present Continuous
Am / Is / Are + subject + -ing		Algo acontecendo agora. Something happening now.Are you enjoying the fair?
Present Perfect
Have / Has + subject + past participle		Experiência e duração até agora. Experience and duration up to now.Have you been here before?
Question wordsWhat (o que) · Where (onde) · How long (quanto tempo) · Why (por que) · Who (quem)

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

" you come to this fair every year?"
" you enjoying the panels so far?"
"How long you worked in hospitality?"
"I just arrived from Brazil."
"What you to the fair this year?"
" you been to the keynote yet?"

Stage 2: Put the Conversation in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de uma conversa de small talk na feira na ordem correta.

?Break the ice with a friendly opener.
?Wrap up and exchange cards.
?Ask a follow-up question to learn more.
?Find some common ground.
?Introduce yourself and say what you do.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

Hi there! Are you enjoying the fair so far?
Oi! Você está gostando da feira até agora?
So, what brings you to the fair this year?
Então, o que te traz à feira este ano?
How long have you worked in hospitality?
Há quanto tempo você trabalha com hotelaria?
It sounds like we have a lot in common.
Parece que temos muito em comum.
It was great to chat. Let us keep in touch.
Foi ótimo conversar. Vamos manter contato.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

A delegate stops by your booth. The best icebreaker is:
A You are enjoying the fair?
B Hi there! Are you enjoying the fair so far?
C Why you are here?
You want to ask about someone's experience. Which is correct?
A How long you work in hospitality?
B How long have you worked in hospitality?
C How long do you working in hospitality?
You want to keep the conversation going. The best follow-up question is:
A Okay, bye now.
B That is interesting! What brings you to the fair this year?
C I do not have more questions.
You want to end the chat warmly. You say:
A It was great to chat. Let us keep in touch.
B We finish now, goodbye.
C You are boring, I go.

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine you are at an international tourism fair and a stranger walks up to your booth. Start a conversation: break the ice, introduce yourself, ask two follow-up questions, and find some common ground. Use at least three questions with Do, Are, or Have. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 3

1Hi there! Are you enjoying the fair so far?Oi! Você está gostando da feira até agora?
2So, what brings you to the fair this year?Então, o que te traz à feira este ano?
3How long have you worked in hospitality?Há quanto tempo você trabalha com hotelaria?
4It sounds like we have a lot in common.Parece que temos muito em comum.
5It was great to chat. Let us keep in touch.Foi ótimo conversar. Vamos manter contato.
Aula 04 -- Pre-class

Talking About Your World -- Trips & Highlights

Describing past experiences, trips and professional highlights. Key words: highlight, achievement, challenge, outcome, to turn out, to end up, tour operator, contract negotiation, hotel inspection, fam trip, rates, commission. Structures: past simple (regular and irregular verbs) and past questions with 'did'.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Highlight -- the best or most memorable part of an experience (ponto alto, destaque)
"The highlight of the trip was the hotel inspection."
Achievement -- something good you succeeded in doing (conquista, realização)
"Signing that contract was a real achievement."
Challenge -- a difficult task that tests your skills (desafio)
"The biggest challenge was the tight schedule."
Outcome -- the final result of an action or event (resultado)
"The outcome of the meeting was very positive."
To turn out -- to happen in a particular way in the end (acabar sendo, resultar)
"The trip turned out better than expected."
To end up -- to finally be in a situation or place (acabar em, terminar)
"We ended up signing three new contracts."
Tour operator -- a company that creates and sells travel packages (operadora de turismo)
"We partnered with a local tour operator."
Contract negotiation -- the discussion to agree the terms of a deal (negociação de contrato)
"The contract negotiation took two days."
Hotel inspection -- a visit to check a hotel's quality (vistoria de hotel)
"I did a hotel inspection before signing."
Fam trip -- a familiarization trip for agents to experience a destination (viagem de familiarização)
"We joined a fam trip to Portugal."
Rates -- the prices a hotel charges (tarifas, diárias)
"We agreed on special rates for next season."
Commission -- the percentage a company earns on each sale (comissão)
"The commission on that package is fifteen percent."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Highlight
Achievement
Challenge
Outcome
To turn out
To end up
Tour operator
Contract negotiation
Hotel inspection
Fam trip
Rates
Commission

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Last November, Andrea attended WTM in London. She went with a clear goal: to find new hotel partners. The first two days were a real challenge, because the halls were huge and the schedule was tight. On the third day, she met Ricardo, a tour operator from Lisbon. He invited her on a fam trip to Portugal, where she did a full hotel inspection of six properties. The contract negotiation was tough, but in the end it turned out perfectly: they agreed on great rates and a fair commission. Andrea ended up signing three new contracts. The highlight, she says, was the moment she realized her English no longer got in the way.

1. Why do we say "Last November, Andrea attended WTM"?
A Because it is a finished action at a finished time (past simple).
B Because it is happening right now.
C Because it is a routine she repeats every week.
2. "She met Ricardo" uses 'met' (not 'meeted') because:
A The verb 'meet' is regular and adds -ed.
B The verb 'meet' is irregular, so its past form changes completely.
C It is a present tense verb.
3. Which past question is correct?
A Did you signed the contract?
B Did you sign the contract?
C Did you signing the contract?

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The Past Simple

GRAMMAR

Como contar uma história no passado com a forma verbal certa (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
Regular verbs
verb + -ed		Ação terminada, tempo terminado. Finished action, finished time.We signed the contract.
Irregular verbs
special past form		Verbos comuns mudam completamente. Common verbs change.I went / met / did / flew.
Questions & negatives
did + base verb		Use 'did' + verbo na base (sem -ed). Use 'did' + base form.Did you meet them?
Common irregularsgo → went · meet → met · do → did · fly → flew · take → took · be → was/were

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"Last November, I a fair in London."
"On the third day, I a tour operator."
"I on a fam trip to Portugal."
" you negotiate good rates?"
"The negotiation out perfectly."
"In the end, we three contracts."

Stage 2: Put the Conversation in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de contar a história de uma viagem na ordem correta.

?Tell the highlight of the trip.
?Share the final outcome.
?Describe the biggest challenge.
?Explain what your goal was.
?Say where you went and when.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

Last November, I attended a big fair in London.
Em novembro passado, participei de uma grande feira em Londres.
The highlight was signing a new contract.
O ponto alto foi assinar um novo contrato.
The biggest challenge was the tight schedule.
O maior desafio foi a agenda apertada.
In the end, it turned out better than expected.
No fim, acabou melhor do que o esperado.
I ended up with three new partners.
Acabei com três novos parceiros.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

A colleague asks: "Where did you go last month?" You answer:
A I go to Portugal last month.
B I went to Portugal last month.
C I am going to Portugal last month.
You want to ask about a past deal. Which is correct?
A Did you signed the contract?
B Did you sign the contract?
C Do you signed the contract?
You want to describe how a hard trip finished well. You say:
A It was tough at first, but in the end it turned out perfectly.
B It is tough, but it turns out perfectly.
C It will be tough, but it turned out perfectly.
You want to share a professional achievement. The best sentence is:
A I ended up with three new partners after the trip.
B I ending up with three new partners after the trip.
C I end up with three new partners after the trip.

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine a colleague asks about a real professional trip or fair you attended. Tell the whole story in the past simple: where you went, what you did, who you met, the biggest challenge, and the final outcome. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 4

1Last November, I attended a big fair in London.Em novembro passado, participei de uma grande feira em Londres.
2The highlight was signing a new contract.O ponto alto foi assinar um novo contrato.
3The biggest challenge was the tight schedule.O maior desafio foi a agenda apertada.
4In the end, it turned out better than expected.No fim, acabou melhor do que o esperado.
5I ended up with three new partners.Acabei com três novos parceiros.
Aula 05 -- Pre-class

What's Next -- Plans, Goals & Commitments

Talking about plans, goals and future commitments in professional contexts. Key words: to arrange, to schedule, to confirm, upcoming, to look forward to, commitment, MICE segment, corporate travel, luxury travel, all-inclusive, charter flight, ground operator. Structures: future forms -- 'going to' (plans), 'will' (decisions and offers), and present continuous for fixed arrangements.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

To arrange -- to plan and organize something in advance (organizar, agendar)
"I will arrange a meeting for next week."
To schedule -- to fix a time for an event (marcar, agendar)
"We are going to schedule the fam trip for March."
To confirm -- to say officially that something is certain (confirmar)
"I will confirm the rates by email."
Upcoming -- happening soon, in the near future (próximo, que está por vir)
"Our upcoming fair is in Berlin."
To look forward to -- to feel happy about something that will happen (aguardar com expectativa)
"I look forward to working with you."
Commitment -- a promise or a plan you are sure about (compromisso)
"We made a firm commitment to expand."
MICE segment -- meetings, incentives, conferences and events travel (segmento de eventos corporativos)
"The MICE segment is growing fast."
Corporate travel -- business trips organized for companies (viagens corporativas)
"We are launching a corporate travel program."
Luxury travel -- high-end, premium travel experiences (turismo de luxo)
"Luxury travel is our fastest-growing area."
All-inclusive -- a package where meals and drinks are included (tudo incluído)
"Many clients prefer an all-inclusive package."
Charter flight -- a plane hired for a specific group or route (voo fretado)
"We are arranging a charter flight for the group."
Ground operator -- a local company that handles services at a destination (operador local, receptivo)
"Our ground operator will confirm the transfers."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

To arrange
To schedule
To confirm
Upcoming
To look forward to
Commitment
MICE segment
Corporate travel
Luxury travel
All-inclusive
Charter flight
Ground operator

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Andrea is planning next season with a partner named Henrik. "We are going to expand our corporate travel program," he says, "and we would love you for the MICE segment." Andrea already has some fixed plans. "We are attending ITB in March," she replies, "so let us schedule a meeting there." Henrik makes an offer on the spot: "Before that, I will send you our upcoming rates and a draft agreement." Andrea agrees. "If everything looks good, we will confirm the partnership and arrange a fam trip in April." It is a firm commitment from both sides. "I really look forward to working with you," Andrea says.

1. Why does Henrik say "We are going to expand our program"?
A Because it is a plan they decided before now (going to).
B Because he is deciding it at this exact moment.
C Because it already happened in the past.
2. "We are attending ITB in March" uses the present continuous because:
A It is a spontaneous decision made right now.
B It is a fixed arrangement already booked.
C It is a general fact that is always true.
3. Which sentence is a decision or offer made in the moment?
A I will send you the upcoming rates.
B We are going to expand next year.
C We are attending ITB in March.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Future Forms: going to / will / present continuous

GRAMMAR

Como falar de planos, decisões e agendas com a forma de futuro certa (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
going to
am/is/are going to + verb		Planos e intenções decididos antes de agora. Plans and intentions.We are going to expand.
will
will + base verb		Decisões, ofertas e promessas feitas agora. Decisions, offers, promises.I will send the rates.
Present Continuous
am/is/are + -ing		Compromissos fixos já marcados. Fixed arrangements already booked.We are attending ITB.
look forward toDepois de 'look forward to', use o verbo em -ing. After 'look forward to', use -ing.I look forward to working with you.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"Perfect, I send you the rates after the fair."
"Our plan is clear: we expand our portfolio."
"It is already booked: we ITB in March."
"I look to working with you."
"Let us a meeting for next week."
"Do not worry, I confirm the rates today."

Stage 2: Put the Conversation in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de uma conversa de planejamento na ordem correta.

?Schedule a meeting to discuss the details.
?Say which fair you are already attending.
?Share your main goal for next season.
?Offer to send the upcoming rates.
?Confirm the partnership and next steps.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

Next season, we are going to expand our portfolio.
Na próxima temporada, vamos expandir o nosso portfólio.
We are attending ITB in March.
Vamos participar da ITB em março.
I will send you the rates by the end of the week.
Vou te enviar as tarifas até o fim da semana.
Let us schedule a meeting and confirm the details.
Vamos marcar uma reunião e confirmar os detalhes.
I look forward to working with you.
Estou ansiosa para trabalhar com você.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

A partner asks about your plans. The best answer is:
A We will expanding our portfolio next season.
B We are going to expand our portfolio next season.
C We are going to expanding our portfolio next season.
You want to talk about a fair that is already booked. Which is correct?
A We will attending ITB in March.
B We are attending ITB in March.
C We attend ITB in March, maybe.
A partner asks you to send the rates. You make an offer on the spot:
A Of course, I will send them by Friday.
B Of course, I am sending them last week.
C Of course, I sending them by Friday.
You want to end the call warmly. You say:
A I look forward to work with you.
B I look forward to working with you.
C I look forward work with you.

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine a partner asks about your professional plans for the next three months. Describe your goals using 'going to', mention a fair you are already attending (present continuous), and make one offer or promise with 'will'. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 5

1Next season, we are going to expand our portfolio.Na próxima temporada, vamos expandir o nosso portfólio.
2We are attending ITB in March.Vamos participar da ITB em março.
3I will send you the rates by the end of the week.Vou te enviar as tarifas até o fim da semana.
4Let us schedule a meeting and confirm the details.Vamos marcar uma reunião e confirmar os detalhes.
5I look forward to working with you.Estou ansiosa para trabalhar com você.
Aula 06 -- Pre-class

Making It Stick -- Follow-Ups & Relationships

Networking language, follow-ups and long-term relationships. Key words: to stay in touch, to circle back, to keep someone posted, mutual benefit, long-term relationship, business card, rate sheet, product presentation, preferred partner, exclusive deal, room block, volume agreement. Structures: polite modal verbs -- 'could' (requests), 'would' (offers), and 'should' (recommendations), always followed by the base verb.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

To stay in touch -- to keep contact with someone over time (manter contato)
"Let us stay in touch after the fair."
To circle back -- to return to a topic or contact someone again later (retomar depois)
"I will circle back next week with the details."
To keep someone posted -- to keep someone informed of news (manter alguém informado)
"I will keep you posted on the dates."
Mutual benefit -- an advantage for both sides (benefício mútuo)
"This deal is a mutual benefit for us both."
Long-term relationship -- a professional connection that lasts for years (relacionamento de longo prazo)
"We are building a long-term relationship."
Business card -- a small card with your name and contact details (cartão de visita)
"Here is my business card."
Rate sheet -- a document listing a hotel's prices (tabela de tarifas)
"Could you send me your rate sheet?"
Product presentation -- a talk that introduces what a company offers (apresentação de produto)
"We would love to give you a product presentation."
Preferred partner -- a company you work with first, by agreement (parceiro preferencial)
"You are now our preferred partner in Brazil."
Exclusive deal -- an offer only one partner receives (acordo exclusivo)
"We can offer you an exclusive deal."
Room block -- a group of rooms reserved for one client or event (bloqueio de quartos)
"We should reserve a room block for the group."
Volume agreement -- a deal based on selling a large number of bookings (acordo por volume)
"A volume agreement gives you better rates."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

To stay in touch
To circle back
To keep someone posted
Mutual benefit
Long-term relationship
Business card
Rate sheet
Product presentation
Preferred partner
Exclusive deal
Room block
Volume agreement

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Two days after the fair, Andrea writes a follow-up to Paulo, a partner she met in Portugal. "It was a pleasure to meet you," she begins. "Could you send me your rate sheet for next season?" Paulo replies warmly. "Of course. I would love to give you a short product presentation, too." Andrea sees the opportunity. "There is a real mutual benefit here. You should visit our destination in high season." Paulo agrees and offers to make Escape Turismo a preferred partner with an exclusive deal. "Let us stay in touch," Andrea says. "I will keep you posted and circle back with the details. This is the start of a great long-term relationship."

1. Why does Andrea say "Could you send me your rate sheet?" instead of "Send me the rate sheet"?
A Because 'could' makes the request polite and professional.
B Because 'could' talks about the past.
C Because 'could' is only used for ability.
2. "You should visit our destination in high season" is:
A A polite request.
B A recommendation or piece of advice.
C A promise about the past.
3. Which sentence correctly follows a modal with the base verb?
A I would love to help you.
B You should to visit us.
C Could you to send the rates?

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Polite Modals: could / would / should

GRAMMAR

Como fazer pedidos, ofertas e recomendações com educação (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
could + base verbPedido educado ou sugestão leve. A polite request or soft suggestion.Could you send the rates?
would + base verbOferta calorosa ou desejo profissional. A warm offer or wish.I would love to help.
should + base verbRecomendação ou conselho. A recommendation or advice.You should visit in high season.
Regra de ouro / Golden ruleDepois de could, would e should, use o verbo na base, SEM 'to'. After a modal, use the base verb, no 'to'.should visit (not should to visit)

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

" you send me your rate sheet?"
"I love to schedule a product presentation."
"You visit our destination in high season."
"Let us in touch after the fair."
"I will you posted on the dates."
"There is a real here for both companies."

Stage 2: Put the Conversation in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de uma mensagem de follow-up na ordem correta.

?Explain the mutual benefit of working together.
?Make a polite request for the rate sheet.
?Offer a product presentation.
?Agree to stay in touch and keep them posted.
?Thank the person for meeting you at the fair.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

It was a pleasure to meet you at the fair.
Foi um prazer conhecer você na feira.
Could you send me your rate sheet?
Você poderia me enviar a sua tabela de tarifas?
I would love to schedule a call.
Eu adoraria marcar uma ligação.
You should visit our destination in high season.
Você deveria visitar o nosso destino na alta temporada.
Let us stay in touch. I will keep you posted.
Vamos manter contato. Eu vou te manter informado.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

You want to ask a partner for their rate sheet, politely. You say:
A Send me the rate sheet now.
B Could you send me your rate sheet?
C Could you to send me your rate sheet?
You want to make a warm offer to a partner. The best choice is:
A I would love to give you a product presentation.
B I would loved to give you a product presentation.
C I will love giving you a product presentation.
A partner asks when to visit. You recommend high season. You say:
A You should to visit in high season.
B You should visiting in high season.
C You should visit in high season.
You want to end the message warmly and keep the door open. You say:
A Let us stay in touch. I will keep you posted.
B Let us stay in touch. I will keeping you posted.
C Let us to stay in touch. I keep you posted.

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine you met a great supplier at a fair two days ago. Record a short follow-up message. Thank them, make a polite request with 'could', a warm offer with 'would', and a recommendation with 'should', and agree to stay in touch. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 6

1It was a pleasure to meet you at the fair.Foi um prazer conhecer você na feira.
2Could you send me your rate sheet?Você poderia me enviar a sua tabela de tarifas?
3I would love to schedule a call.Eu adoraria marcar uma ligação.
4You should visit our destination in high season.Você deveria visitar o nosso destino na alta temporada.
5Let us stay in touch. I will keep you posted.Vamos manter contato. Eu vou te manter informado.
Aula 07 -- Pre-class

Listening and Responding -- Understanding Native Speakers

Understanding fast native speakers and clarifying in real time. Key words: to catch, connected speech, native speaker, accent, to lose the thread, to follow along, filler word, to paraphrase, to rephrase, background noise, the gist, to speak up. Structures: clarification language -- 'Would you mind + -ing?', 'Do you mean...?', and 'So you are saying...?' to check and confirm understanding.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

To catch -- to hear and understand what someone says (captar/entender)
"Sorry, I did not catch that."
Connected speech -- the way words blend together in fast speech (fala conectada)
"Connected speech makes fast English hard to follow."
Native speaker -- a person whose first language is English (falante nativo)
"The buyer was a native speaker from Texas."
Accent -- the way people from a region pronounce words (sotaque)
"Her Australian accent was new to me."
To lose the thread -- to stop following a conversation (perder o fio)
"I lost the thread for a moment."
To follow along -- to understand while someone is speaking (acompanhar)
"I could follow along most of the time."
Filler word -- a small word like well that gives you thinking time (palavra de preenchimento)
"Filler words like well give you a second to think."
To paraphrase -- to repeat an idea in different words (parafrasear)
"Let me paraphrase what you just said."
To rephrase -- to say something in a different way (reformular)
"Could you rephrase the question?"
Background noise -- sounds that make listening harder (ruído de fundo)
"There was a lot of background noise at the booth."
The gist -- the general meaning of what someone said (a essência)
"I got the gist of the offer."
To speak up -- to talk more loudly and clearly (falar mais alto)
"Could you speak up a little, please?"

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

To catch
Connected speech
Native speaker
Accent
To lose the thread
To follow along
Filler word
To paraphrase
To rephrase
Background noise
The gist
To speak up

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

At a crowded fair, Andrea meets David, a fast-talking buyer from Lisbon. His accent is new to her and the background noise is loud. For a second she starts to lose the thread. Instead of panicking, she smiles and says, 'Would you mind repeating that?' David slows down. Andrea listens for the gist, not every word, so she can follow along. Then she checks the details: 'Do you mean the group rate or the net rate?' Finally, she confirms in her own words: 'So you are saying the offer is only for the low season?' David nods. By asking to rephrase and to paraphrase, Andrea never loses control of the conversation.

1. Why does Andrea say 'Would you mind repeating that?' instead of staying silent?
A To politely ask David to say it again so she does not lose the thread.
B Because she wants to change the subject.
C Because the deal is already finished.
2. 'So you are saying the offer is only for the low season?' is used to:
A make a complaint.
B confirm the message in her own words.
C end the conversation.
3. After 'Would you mind', the correct form is:
A the -ing form: 'Would you mind repeating that?'
B the base verb: 'Would you mind repeat that?'
C 'to' + verb: 'Would you mind to repeat that?'

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Clarifying and Checking Understanding

GRAMMAR

Como pedir para repetir e confirmar que você entendeu (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
Would you mind + -ing?Pedido educado para repetir ou ir mais devagar. Politely ask to repeat or slow down.Would you mind repeating that?
Do you mean...?Confere qual opção a pessoa quis dizer. Check which option the speaker means.Do you mean today or tomorrow?
So you are saying...?Confirma a mensagem com suas palavras. Confirm the message in your own words.So you are saying it includes breakfast?
Regra de ouro / Golden ruleDepois de 'Would you mind', use o verbo com -ing, SEM 'to'. After 'Would you mind', use the -ing form, no 'to'.would you mind repeating (not to repeat)

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

" you mind repeating that?"
"Would you mind the last part?"
" you mean the group rate?"
"So you saying it is for groups only?"
"Sorry, I did not that."
"Could you up a little, please?"

Stage 2: Put the Conversation in Order

Order

Coloque os passos para lidar com um falante rápido na ordem correta.

?Say 'Would you mind repeating that?' politely.
?Listen for the gist, not every single word.
?Paraphrase to confirm: 'So you are saying...?'
?If you lose the thread, ask them to slow down.
?Check the details with 'Do you mean...?'

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

Sorry, I did not catch that.
Desculpe, não entendi.
Would you mind repeating that?
Você se importaria de repetir?
Could you speak more slowly, please?
Você poderia falar mais devagar?
Do you mean the group rate or the net rate?
Você quer dizer a tarifa de grupo ou a líquida?
So you are saying it is only for the low season?
Então é só para a baixa temporada?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

A buyer speaks too fast and you miss the price. You say:
A Speak slow!
B Would you mind repeating that, please?
C Would you mind to repeat that?
You are not sure which rate the partner means. You ask:
A Do you mean the net rate or the group rate?
B You mean what?
C Repeat the rate.
You want to confirm what you heard. You say:
A I not understand.
B So you are saying the deal is only for the low season?
C So you saying the deal?
The line is noisy and the voice is low. You say:
A Could you speak up a little, please?
B Talk more loud!
C Speak up you please?

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine a native speaker is talking fast at a fair and you miss part of the price. Record a short reply: politely ask them to repeat or slow down, check which option they mean, and paraphrase to confirm you understood. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 7

1Sorry, I did not catch that.Desculpe, não entendi essa parte.
2Would you mind repeating that?Você se importaria de repetir?
3Could you speak more slowly, please?Você poderia falar mais devagar, por favor?
4Do you mean the group rate or the net rate?Você quer dizer a tarifa de grupo ou a tarifa líquida?
5So you are saying it is only for the low season?Então você está dizendo que é só para a baixa temporada?
Aula 08 -- Pre-class

Midpoint Review -- How Far Have You Come?

A midpoint review of your English journey. Key words: progress, milestone, confidence, fluency, comfort zone, to keep up with, strength, area for improvement, breakthrough, to take stock, self-assessment, halfway point. Structures: past simple, present continuous, and future forms (going to / will) used together to describe your journey.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Progress -- steady improvement over time (progresso)
"I can see real progress in my speaking."
Milestone -- an important point you reach (marco)
"This class is an important milestone."
Confidence -- the feeling of being sure of yourself (confiança)
"My confidence has grown a lot."
Fluency -- speaking smoothly and easily (fluência)
"Fluency matters more than perfection."
Comfort zone -- the easy, familiar situation (zona de conforto)
"Speaking English pushes me out of my comfort zone."
To keep up with -- to stay at the same level as something (acompanhar)
"I can keep up with fast conversations now."
Strength -- something you are good at (ponto forte)
"Small talk is now a real strength."
Area for improvement -- something you still need to work on (ponto a melhorar)
"Pronunciation is my main area for improvement."
Breakthrough -- a sudden, important step forward (avanço)
"Last week I had a real breakthrough."
To take stock -- to review your situation carefully (fazer um balanço)
"It is a good moment to take stock."
Self-assessment -- evaluating your own work honestly (autoavaliação)
"This lesson is a self-assessment."
Halfway point -- the middle of a journey (meio do caminho)
"We are at the halfway point of the program."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Progress
Milestone
Confidence
Fluency
Comfort zone
To keep up with
Strength
Area for improvement
Breakthrough
To take stock
Self-assessment
Halfway point

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Today is a special class: the halfway point of the program. Andrea decides to take stock. Six months ago, she started the course and could barely speak in a meeting. Right now, she is improving her fluency every week and can keep up with fast speakers. Her confidence has grown, and small talk is now a real strength. She still has one area for improvement: pronunciation. But she can see clear progress and a real breakthrough. Next month, she is going to present Escape Turismo at a fair in Berlin, all in English.

1. 'Six months ago, she started the course' uses the past simple because:
A it describes a finished action in the past.
B it is happening right now.
C it is a future plan.
2. 'Right now, she is improving her fluency' is in the present continuous because:
A it is a finished past action.
B it describes something in progress now.
C it is a prediction.
3. Which sentence correctly talks about a future plan?
A Next month, she is going to present in Berlin.
B Next month, she presented in Berlin.
C Next month, she is present in Berlin.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Past, Present, and Future Together

GRAMMAR

Como falar do passado, do presente e do futuro na mesma conversa (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
Past simpleAção terminada no passado. A finished past action.Six months ago, I started.
Present continuousAção em progresso agora. An action in progress now.Right now, I am improving.
going to / willPlano ou previsão para o futuro. A plan or prediction.Next month, I am going to present.
Regra de ouro / Golden ruleA palavra de tempo escolhe o tempo verbal: 'ago' -> passado; 'right now' -> presente contínuo; 'next' -> futuro.ago -> started; now -> am improving; next -> am going to

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"Six months ago, I this program."
"Right now, I improving my fluency."
"Next month, I am to present at a fair."
"I can see real this year."
"It is a good moment to take ."
"My main is small talk."

Stage 2: Put the Conversation in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de uma boa autoavaliação na ordem correta.

?Name one area for improvement.
?Describe what you are working on right now.
?Share your plan for the future.
?Name one clear strength.
?Look back: say where you started.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

Six months ago, I could not speak in meetings.
Há seis meses, eu não conseguia falar em reuniões.
Right now, I am improving my fluency.
Agora, estou melhorando minha fluência.
I have made real progress this year.
Eu fiz um progresso real este ano.
My main area for improvement is pronunciation.
Meu principal ponto a melhorar é a pronúncia.
Next month, I am going to present at a fair.
No próximo mês, vou apresentar em uma feira.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

You want to describe a finished past action. You say:
A Six months ago, I started this course.
B Six months ago, I am starting this course.
C Six months ago, I start this course.
You want to describe what you are doing now. You say:
A Right now, I improve my fluency.
B Right now, I am improving my fluency.
C Right now, I improved my fluency.
You want to talk about a future plan. You say:
A Next month, I am going to present in Berlin.
B Next month, I present in Berlin yesterday.
C Next month, I am present in Berlin.
You want to take stock honestly. You say:
A My strength is small talk, and my area for improvement is pronunciation.
B My strength small talk, my improvement pronunciation.
C I am strength small talk.

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Take stock of your English journey. Record a short reflection: what could you not do six months ago, what are you working on right now, and what are you going to do in the second half of the program? Use past, present, and future. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 8

1Six months ago, I could not speak in meetings.Há seis meses, eu não conseguia falar em reuniões.
2Right now, I am improving my fluency.Agora, estou melhorando minha fluência.
3I have made real progress this year.Eu fiz um progresso real este ano.
4My main area for improvement is pronunciation.Meu principal ponto a melhorar é a pronúncia.
5Next month, I am going to present at a fair.No próximo mês, vou apresentar em uma feira.
Aula 09 -- Pre-class

Talking Prices, Packages, and Deals

How to quote prices and negotiate deals in tourism. Key words: net rate, markup, group rate, deposit, cancellation policy, peak season, low season, package, surcharge, to quote, complimentary, discount. Structure: the first conditional (if + present simple, will + verb) to trade a condition for an offer.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Net rate -- the base price before any markup is added (tarifa líquida)
"The hotel gave me a good net rate."
Markup -- the extra amount added to the base price to make a profit (margem)
"We add a small markup to the net rate."
Group rate -- a special reduced price for a large number of people (tarifa de grupo)
"Can you offer a group rate for forty guests?"
Deposit -- money paid in advance to hold a booking (depósito, sinal)
"We ask for a twenty percent deposit."
Cancellation policy -- the rules for changing or cancelling a booking (política de cancelamento)
"What is your cancellation policy?"
Peak season -- the busiest and most expensive time of the year (alta temporada)
"Prices rise in peak season."
Low season -- the quietest and cheapest time of the year (baixa temporada)
"The same room costs less in the low season."
Package -- several services combined and sold at one price (pacote)
"This package includes hotel and transfers."
Surcharge -- an extra charge added to the basic price (taxa extra)
"A small surcharge applies at the weekend."
To quote -- to state the price for a service (cotar, orçar)
"Could you quote me a rate for October?"
Complimentary -- free, included at no extra cost (cortesia, grátis)
"Breakfast is complimentary."
Discount -- a reduction in the normal price (desconto)
"If you book early, I will offer a discount."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Net rate
Markup
Group rate
Deposit
Cancellation policy
Peak season
Low season
Package
Surcharge
To quote
Complimentary
Discount

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Andrea is preparing a quote for an important client. The hotel gave her a net rate, and she adds a small markup before sending the package to the client. Because the trip is in peak season, a weekend surcharge applies, but Andrea knows how to negotiate. 'If you book more than forty rooms, I will hold the low-season rate,' she says. She also explains the deposit and the cancellation policy clearly. To close the deal, she adds that breakfast is complimentary. The client is happy, and both sides feel they have won.

1. 'If you book more than forty rooms, I will hold the rate.' The 'if' part uses:
A the present simple (book).
B will + verb.
C the past simple.
2. Which sentence is correct?
A If you will pay the deposit, breakfast is free.
B If you pay the deposit, breakfast will be free.
C If you paying the deposit, breakfast free.
3. We use the first conditional to talk about:
A a real future possibility and its result.
B something that already happened.
C an impossible dream.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The First Conditional

GRAMMAR

Como negociar em inglês: 'se... então...' (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
If + present simpleA condição. The condition -- o que precisa acontecer primeiro.If you book early,
will + verbO resultado. The result -- o que você promete.I will give a discount.
Nunca 'will' depois de 'if' / Never 'will' after ifMantenha o presente simples na parte do 'if'.If you pay (não 'will pay')
Regra de ouro / Golden ruleif + presente simples, vírgula, will + verbo.If you book, I will offer a rate.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"If you early, I will offer a discount."
"If you pay the deposit, breakfast be complimentary."
"Could you me a group rate?"
"A small applies in peak season."
"What is your cancellation ?"
"Breakfast is ."

Stage 2: Put the Conversation in Order

Order

Coloque as etapas de uma boa negociação de preço na ordem correta.

?Confirm the deal and thank the client.
?Ask about the client's needs (group size and dates).
?Trade a condition for an offer using 'if... I will...'.
?Explain the deposit and the cancellation policy.
?Quote the net rate and the package.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

Could you quote me a group rate?
Você poderia me passar uma tarifa de grupo?
If you book early, I will offer a discount.
Se você reservar cedo, eu ofereço um desconto.
A small surcharge applies in peak season.
Uma pequena taxa extra se aplica na alta temporada.
Breakfast is complimentary.
O café da manhã é cortesia.
What is your cancellation policy?
Qual é a sua política de cancelamento?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

A client asks for a discount. You say:
A If you book forty rooms, I will offer a better rate.
B If you will book forty rooms, I offer a better rate.
C You book forty rooms, discount.
You want to explain the deposit. You say:
A We ask for a twenty percent deposit to confirm.
B We ask a deposit twenty percent confirm.
C Deposit twenty for confirm percent.
It is peak season and a surcharge applies. You say:
A A small surcharge applies at the weekend in peak season.
B A small surcharge apply weekend peak.
C Peak season is surcharge always applying.
You want to close the deal warmly. You say:
A If you sign today, breakfast will be complimentary.
B If you signing today, breakfast complimentary.
C You sign today breakfast free will.

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine a client wants a group package for peak season but says your price is too high. Record a short negotiation: quote your rate, then use the first conditional to trade at least two conditions for offers (for example, 'If you book early, I will...'). Explain the deposit and what is included. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 9

1Could you quote me a group rate?Você poderia me passar uma tarifa de grupo?
2If you book early, I will offer a discount.Se você reservar cedo, eu ofereço um desconto.
3A small surcharge applies in peak season.Uma pequena taxa extra se aplica na alta temporada.
4Breakfast is complimentary.O café da manhã é cortesia.
5What is your cancellation policy?Qual é a sua política de cancelamento?
Aula 10 -- Pre-class

Advising Clients and Handling Tricky Requests

How to advise clients and handle difficult requests in tourism. Key words: alternative, to recommend, upgrade, flexible, trade-off, downside, to weigh up, peace of mind, tailor-made, worthwhile, to reconsider, second thoughts. Structure: the second conditional (if + past simple, would/could + verb) to give hypothetical advice.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Alternative -- another option you can choose instead (alternativa)
"Let me suggest a smart alternative."
To recommend -- to suggest something is a good choice (recomendar)
"I would recommend the inland hotel."
Upgrade -- a better room or service than the one booked (upgrade, melhoria)
"I could offer a free upgrade."
Flexible -- able to change or adapt easily (flexível)
"The tailor-made package is very flexible."
Trade-off -- a balance between two things you cannot both have fully (contrapartida)
"There is a trade-off: cheaper, but further away."
Downside -- the negative side of something (desvantagem)
"The downside is the longer transfer."
To weigh up -- to consider the good and bad points carefully (ponderar)
"Let us weigh up the two options."
Peace of mind -- a calm feeling from knowing nothing will go wrong (tranquilidade)
"The insurance gives you peace of mind."
Tailor-made -- specially designed for one client's needs (feito sob medida)
"We offer tailor-made itineraries."
Worthwhile -- good enough to be worth the time or money (que vale a pena)
"The extra cost is worthwhile."
To reconsider -- to think about a decision again (reconsiderar)
"You might want to reconsider the dates."
Second thoughts -- new doubts about a decision (dúvidas, hesitação)
"I am having second thoughts about August."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Alternative
To recommend
Upgrade
Flexible
Trade-off
Downside
To weigh up
Peace of mind
Tailor-made
Worthwhile
To reconsider
Second thoughts

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Andrea's client, Stefan, has a tricky request: a luxury trip on a small budget. Andrea never simply says no. Instead, she offers a smart alternative. 'If I were you, I would travel in the low season,' she says. She explains the trade-off clearly: the inland hotel is cheaper, but the downside is that it is further from the beach. She helps Stefan weigh up the options and recommends a tailor-made package for peace of mind. In the end, Stefan agrees it is worthwhile -- and he trusts Andrea even more.

1. 'If I were you, I would travel in the low season' uses:
A the second conditional for hypothetical advice.
B the first conditional for a real plan.
C the past simple for a finished action.
2. In the second conditional, after 'I' we use:
A was.
B were (If I were you).
C am.
3. Which sentence gives polite, hypothetical advice?
A If you had more time, you could visit both cities.
B If you have more time, you will visit both cities.
C You must visit both cities.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The Second Conditional

GRAMMAR

Como dar conselhos e falar de situações hipotéticas em inglês (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
If + past simpleA condição imaginária. An unreal condition.If I were you,
would / could + verbO resultado imaginado ou conselho. The imagined result.I would recommend it.
Sempre 'were' / Always 'were'Use 'were' para I/he/she nesta estrutura.If she were here (não 'was')
Regra de ouro / Golden ruleif + passado simples, would/could + verbo (situação irreal).If I were you, I would wait.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"If I you, I would book earlier."
"If you had more time, you visit both cities."
"I would the tailor-made package."
"There is a to consider."
"It would give them peace of ."
"Let me suggest an ."

Stage 2: Put the Conversation in Order

Order

Coloque as etapas de um bom conselho ao cliente na ordem correta.

?Let the client make the final decision.
?Acknowledge it without saying no.
?Explain the trade-offs of each option.
?Offer a smart alternative with 'If I were you...'.
?Listen carefully to the client's request.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesma dizendo-a.

If I were you, I would book earlier.
Se eu fosse você, eu reservaria mais cedo.
I would recommend the tailor-made package.
Eu recomendaria o pacote sob medida.
There is a trade-off to consider.
Há uma contrapartida a considerar.
If you had more time, you could see both cities.
Se você tivesse mais tempo, poderia ver as duas cidades.
Let me suggest a smart alternative.
Deixe-me sugerir uma alternativa inteligente.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação real de trabalho.

A client's request is too expensive. You advise:
A If I were you, I would travel in the low season.
B If I was you, I will travel in the low season.
C You travel low season must.
You want to present a trade-off. You say:
A The inland hotel is cheaper, but it is further from the beach.
B Inland hotel cheaper but far beach is.
C Cheaper hotel beach no problem.
You want to give hypothetical advice politely. You say:
A If you had more time, you could see both cities.
B If you have time, you can see both cities now.
C You see both cities if time.
A client is unsure. You reassure them. You say:
A The tailor-made package would give you peace of mind.
B Tailor package peace mind give you.
C You peace mind package tailor.

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine a client asks you for something almost impossible -- a luxury trip on a very small budget. Record a short piece of advice: acknowledge the request, offer a smart alternative using the second conditional ('If I were you, I would...'), and explain one trade-off clearly. Do not simply say no. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 10

1If I were you, I would book earlier.Se eu fosse você, eu reservaria mais cedo.
2I would recommend the tailor-made package.Eu recomendaria o pacote sob medida.
3There is a trade-off to consider.Há uma contrapartida a considerar.
4If you had more time, you could see both cities.Se você tivesse mais tempo, poderia ver as duas cidades.
5Let me suggest a smart alternative.Deixe-me sugerir uma alternativa inteligente.
Aula 11 -- Pre-class

Handling Complaints and Difficult Situations

How to handle complaints and service problems in tourism. Key words: complaint, to apologize, inconvenience, to resolve, refund, compensation, to reassure, overbooked, goodwill gesture, to escalate, faulty, to follow through. Structure: the passive voice (be + past participle) to sound calm and professional -- the problem is being handled, your refund has been processed.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Complaint -- a formal statement that something is wrong (reclamacao)
"I received a complaint about the rooms."
To apologize -- to say sorry sincerely (pedir desculpas)
"I would like to apologize for the delay."
Inconvenience -- trouble or difficulty caused to someone (transtorno)
"I am so sorry for the inconvenience."
To resolve -- to find a solution to a problem (resolver)
"We will resolve this issue today."
Refund -- money given back to a customer (reembolso)
"A full refund has been processed."
Compensation -- something given to make up for a problem (compensacao)
"The group received compensation for the trouble."
To reassure -- to remove someone's worry or doubt (tranquilizar)
"Let me reassure you: it is being handled."
Overbooked -- with more reservations than places available (com reservas em excesso)
"The hotel was overbooked that night."
Goodwill gesture -- a kind action to keep a customer happy (cortesia, gentileza)
"As a goodwill gesture, dinner is on us."
To escalate -- to pass a serious problem to a manager (encaminhar ao superior)
"I have escalated this to the manager."
Faulty -- not working correctly; defective (com defeito)
"The air conditioning was faulty all week."
To follow through -- to complete what you promised to do (dar seguimento, cumprir)
"I always follow through on a promise."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Complaint
To apologize
Inconvenience
To resolve
Refund
Compensation
To reassure
Overbooked
Goodwill gesture
To escalate
Faulty
To follow through

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Andrea receives a complaint from her partner Lukas: the hotel was overbooked and three rooms were not ready. She never argues. First, she apologizes for the inconvenience. Then she speaks about the problem in the passive voice, to sound calm: 'The rooms were double-booked, and the issue is being handled.' She reassures Lukas that new rooms have been arranged, the first night will be refunded, and the matter has been escalated to the manager. Above all, she follows through -- and Lukas trusts her even more.

1. 'The rooms were double-booked' is an example of:
A the passive voice (be + past participle).
B the second conditional.
C the present continuous active.
2. To describe a problem in progress, the passive is:
A is handling.
B is being handled.
C has handling.
3. Which response sounds calm and professional?
A Your refund has been processed.
B I will process your refund maybe.
C You must wait for the refund.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The Passive Voice

GRAMMAR

Como soar calmo e profissional ao lidar com reclamações (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
be + past participleA ação é feita AO sujeito. The action is done to the subject.The room was booked.
O tempo vem do 'be'is / was / has been / will be + particípio.It has been resolved.
Ação em progresso / In progressis/are being + particípio.It is being handled.
by + agente (opcional)Diga quem fez só se importar. Name who did it only if it matters.It was cancelled by the hotel.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a forma correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"Your refund has been ."
"The problem is being right now."
"New rooms have been at a nearby hotel."
"You will be fully for the trouble."
"The issue has been to the manager."
"I am so sorry for the ."

Stage 2: Put the Conversation in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de uma boa recuperação de serviço na ordem correta.
